mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real retail and food services sales reflects strengthening household consumption, which directly boosts corporate top-line revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between month-over-month RSAFS growth and subsequent SPX 20-day returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces aggregate wage income and consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in nonfarm payrolls and an increase in the unemployment rate capture a genuine labor-market deterioration that neither series alone fully identifies, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In walk-forward testing, the joint signal of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE does not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate is below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, increases in the federal funds rate raise the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and tighten financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months classified as monetary tightening cycles, or the mean return during tightening is not significantly lower than during easing (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansion in industrial production signals rising manufacturing utilization and goods-sector momentum, which lifts near-term earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank information coefficient between INDPRO monthly change and subsequent SPX 5-day returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts sustained above 600 thousand units indicate durable residential investment demand and construction-sector employment strength, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months when HOUST exceeds 600 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices are rising month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains anchored at low levels, the resulting compression in real policy rates drives yield-seeking capital from bonds into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint periods of rising CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS do not produce statistically significant positive average SPX 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real chained GDP captures the broad trajectory of aggregate demand; positive momentum in GDPC1 predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity markets reprice to reflect sustained macro expansion.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth and subsequent SPX 60-day returns exhibit a negative rank correlation over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean return following positive GDP growth is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).
